channel.title: Records of the !mmortal Data Scientist — Writeups
channel.description: Personal writeups — opinions, notes, and reflections that aren’t AI theory.

item.title: Getting Zucked: What Researchers Should Know Before Starting a Company
item.description: Research IP now becomes valuable the moment it works, and researchers are being courted into companies at unprecedented rates. How the sole-owner-entity move runs, step by step, and the rules that stop it.

item.title: San Francisco: The Dreams, the Delulu, and the Messiah Syndrome
item.description: A love letter and an indictment. The city runs on three fuels at once: genuine dreams, collective delusion, and the quiet conviction that you, personally, were sent to save the world.

item.title: Unbacked Applause: Validation, the Unproven, and the Peer Who Was Really a Student
item.description: On seeking validation from people who have never paid for being wrong, the quiet tax of building with the unproven, and why a partnership of equals and a mentorship cannot run on the same relationship without eating it.

item.title: The Universe Is the Better Teacher
item.description: We keep aiming the universal approximator at a single function, human intelligence, and a copy can never surpass its teacher. To exceed us, the model needs a teacher that already exceeds us: the universe and its laws.

item.title: Am I Building the Ship, or the Flood?
item.description: A follow-up to the flood. The doubt that arrives after you pick up the hammer: what if the thing I am building is not the ark, but the rain?

item.title: What Am I, If Not the Things I Make?
item.description: We tied our identity to our craft, and AI is pulling the craft away. But the craft was never the point, and the thing we should fear is not the machine.

item.title: On building spaceships and surviving floods
item.description: When you believe a flood is coming, the only way to survive is to build the ship, even when no one believes you. So you pick up the hammer.

item.title: Why a separate corner for writeups
item.description: The blog is where I prove things, and the payment is a plot. This corner is where I confess things, and the payment is skin. A front door for the essays, the field notes, and the poem.

item.title: Morocco and AI Illiteracy — Part I: The Cost of Staying Behind
item.description: Morocco's AI gap is not a talent problem. It is an education, access, and policy problem — and the cost of falling further behind compounds every year the gap widens.
